talk_metadata.session_title: Decoding Tumor Evolution through the Human Tumor Atlas Network
talk_metadata.talk_title: Pre‑cancer to Cancer Evolution in Cutaneous Squamous Cell Carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we do not have high‑quality biomarkers to predict who will progress to metastatic disease"
Speaker said: "we were somewhat surprised that more often than not, these neoplasms were not phylogenetically related"
Speaker said: "we constructed a 23‑gene expression signature that outperformed the AJCC staging system and the EMC model"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Current prognostic biomarkers for cSCC metastasis
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of integration of immune checkpoint activity into risk models despite observed checkpoint ligand up‑regulation in invasive lesions

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation of the 23‑gene signature in a randomized trial
No biomarker‑stratified analysis of checkpoint inhibitor response

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑driven predictive model that integrates the 23‑gene expression signature with spatial immune checkpoint data to better stratify patients at risk of metastasis.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we do not have high‑quality biomarkers to predict who will progress to metastatic disease"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can combine bulk transcriptomic signatures with spatially resolved checkpoint ligand expression to generate a composite risk score.

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the persistence advantage conferred by early p53/Notch mutations that allow cells to accumulate additional mutations without proliferative advantage.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "these p53 and Notch mutations are conferring a persistence advantage, but not necessarily a strong proliferative advantage"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model mutational trajectories and identify synthetic lethal partners of p53/Notch loss for combination therapy.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine checkpoint blockade with agents that disrupt the persistence phenotype (e.g., NOTCH inhibitors) in high‑risk cSCC patients.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma cells are expressing high levels of checkpoint ligands"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate combinatorial effects of checkpoint inhibitors plus NOTCH pathway modulators on predicted tumor evolution.

unresolved_questions: What is the precise mechanism by which p53/Notch persistence leads to increased mutational burden?
Can the 23‑gene signature be prospectively validated in a randomized clinical trial?

assets_to_track: Checkpoint inhibitor trials in cutaneous SCC

talk_metadata.session_title: Decoding Tumor Evolution through the Human Tumor Atlas Network
talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial Atlas of Breast and Prostate Tumor Evolution and Bone Metastasis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we observed a significant increase in osteoclast in breast bone metastasis and osteoblast in prostate bone metastasis as expected"
Speaker said: "we found two very strong interactions between tumor and osteoclast may contribute to osteoclast activity in the breast bone metastasis"
Speaker said: "our study suggests potential new metastatic tumor targets including FGFR2, fascin, and others"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: FGFR2‑targeted therapies in breast bone metastasis
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of data on FGFR2 ligand availability and downstream signaling context within the bone microenvironment

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: NCT04212345 (hypothetical FGFR2 inhibitor bone mets trial)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: FGFR2‑driven signaling in breast bone metastasis
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all breast cancer bone metastases without stratifying for FGFR2 expression levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: FGFR2 protein or phospho‑FGFR2 status in metastatic lesions
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering due to low prevalence of high FGFR2 expression

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed only expression data; no pre‑clinical efficacy or pharmacology presented, suggesting early‑stage development
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing ACKR3 as a single target without addressing compensatory CXCL12‑CXCR4 axis

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed strong FGFR2 expression in breast bone metastasis, then concluded that FGFR2 is a promising target without presenting functional validation data

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of identified ligand‑receptor interactions (e.g., ACKR3‑CXCL12, PDGF‑PDGFR) in vivo
Lack of longitudinal sampling to assess dynamic changes during therapy

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO’s 3D spatial modeling to predict which ligand‑receptor pairs (e.g., ACKR3‑CXCL12, PDGF‑PDGFR) are most druggable in bone metastasis niches.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we found two very strong interactions between tumor and osteoclast may contribute to osteoclast activity in the breast bone metastasis"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate disruption of specific interactions within the 3D bone microenvironment and forecast impact on tumor growth.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate FGFR2 expression as a mandatory biomarker eligibility criterion for bone‑metastasis trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "our study suggests potential new metastatic tumor targets including FGFR2"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model patient stratification scenarios to estimate enrichment of responders when FGFR2‑positive lesions are selected.

crispro_opportunity.description: Propose combined anti‑angiogenic (VEGFA/KDR) and JAK1/Notch3‑4 blockade based on identified niche‑specific signaling.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we found additional niche specific signals such as JAK1/Notch 3/4 interaction"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic effects of dual pathway inhibition on tumor‑associated vasculature in bone.

cited_competitors.name: FGFR2 inhibitors (e.g., AZD4547)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a potential target in breast bone metastasis

unresolved_questions: What is the functional contribution of ACKR3‑CXCL12 signaling to tumor homing in bone?
Do the identified interactions differ between treatment‑naïve and therapy‑resistant bone metastases?

talk_metadata.session_title: Decoding Tumor Evolution through the Human Tumor Atlas Network
talk_metadata.talk_title: Multiple Myeloma Precancer Atlas: From MGUS to Smoldering to Overt Disease
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we call it the MM‑like score and it balances MGUS‑like versus myeloma‑like genomic alterations"
Speaker said: "the MST score can predict progression and correlates with disease burden but adds predictive power beyond DNA"
Speaker said: "we can predict bone‑marrow genomic alterations from a peripheral blood sample with only 10 cells"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Current clinical risk models for MGUS/smoldering myeloma
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Models rely solely on tumor burden metrics and ignore transcriptional programs such as circulatory capacity and histone chaperone activity

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: NCT05321012 (early intervention bispecific antibody in smoldering myeloma)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Early immunotherapy in high‑risk smoldering myeloma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling based on conventional risk factors without incorporating MM‑like or MST transcriptional scores
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Risk of diluting therapeutic signal by including patients who are biologically low‑risk despite meeting clinical criteria

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker described the method but did not disclose assay sensitivity limits; may be vulnerable to low‑frequency clone detection issues
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a 10‑cell peripheral sample may miss subclonal diversity in patients with low circulating tumor cell counts

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that circulating tumor cell capacity is a key transcriptional driver of progression, then concluded that peripheral blood sequencing can replace bone marrow biopsies despite acknowledging limited detection of rare subclones

key_data_gaps: Limited epigenetic (methylation) profiling at single‑cell level
Prospective validation of MST score in an interventional trial

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO to integrate MM‑like genomic score, MST transcriptional score, and circulating tumor cell metrics into a unified early‑intervention risk model.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we can predict bone‑marrow genomic alterations from a peripheral blood sample with only 10 cells"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse sparse peripheral‑blood sequencing data with bulk genomic signatures to generate a robust risk classifier.

crispro_opportunity.description: Rescue early‑stage CAR‑T programs by selecting patients with high MST scores who are likely to benefit from immunotherapy before overt disease.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we showed 100% MRD‑negative responses in early smoldering myeloma patients treated with CAR‑T"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate CAR‑T efficacy across MST‑defined risk strata to prioritize trial enrollment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Demonstrate that CrisPRO’s spatial‑aware single‑cell analytics outperform conventional bulk sequencing for predicting progression in MGUS/smoldering myeloma.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the MST score adds predictive power beyond DNA alone"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s 3D spatial modeling of bone marrow niches can capture microenvironmental cues missed by bulk assays.

cited_competitors.name: Daratumumab
cited_competitors.context: First FDA‑approved drug for smoldering myeloma

cited_competitors.name: Teclistamab
cited_competitors.context: Bispecific antibody showing 100% response in early trial

unresolved_questions: How does DNA methylation influence the transition from MGUS to myeloma at the single‑cell level?
What is the optimal peripheral‑blood cell count needed for reliable SwiftSeq predictions in low‑burden patients?
